title: The Design Dilemma
concept: Deciding whether a new requirement should be handled by a single complex agent or a team of specialized agents.

exercises.prompt: What should you consider when designing an AI system?

options.text: How to balance tasks among agents
The color of the user interface

exercises.explain: Balancing tasks among agents is crucial for effective design.

cards.title: What is the Design Dilemma?
cards.body: The Design Dilemma is about choosing between using one powerful agent or many smaller, specialized agents to solve a problem.

cards.title: Single Agent vs. Multi-Agent Design
cards.body: A single agent is one complex AI that does everything, while multi-agent design involves several specialized AIs, each doing a specific task.

cards.title: Think of it like a toolbox
cards.body: A single agent is like a Swiss Army knife, doing many functions but sometimes not the best at any one thing. Multi-agent design is like having multiple specialized tools for different jobs, which can be more efficient.

cards.title: Real-World Example
cards.body: In a hospital, a single AI could handle patient care, but separate specialists (like a doctor for surgery, a nurse for care) often deliver better results.

cards.title: Myth: More agents always mean better work
cards.body: It's not always true! Sometimes a single agent can focus better, while other tasks may need teamwork between agents.

cards.title: Visual Comparison
cards.body: See how tasks can be divided between agents.

left.text: One complex AI handles all tasks.

exercises.explain: Multi-Agent Design allows for specialization, which can improve efficiency and effectiveness.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A single agent can always perform better than multiple agents working together.
exercises.explain: It's false because sometimes, many agents working together can solve problems more effectively than one.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: A ___ agent can focus on many tasks at a time, while a multi-agent system can have agents that are specialized.
exercises.sentence: A single agent can focus on many tasks at a time, while a multi-agent system can have agents that are specialized.
exercises.explain: The word 'single' refers directly to the single agent, which takes on multiple tasks.

exercises.prompt: Identify the AI-generated option: Which statement best explains multi-agent systems?

options.text: Multi-agent systems use one AI to solve problems as a group.
Each agent in a multi-agent system specializes in a specific task, working together for better results.
Multi-agent systems are always slower than single agent systems.

exercises.explain: The correct statement highlights how each agent in a multi-agent system focuses on specific tasks, enhancing teamwork.

exercises.prompt: Which design would be best for managing a smart city (traffic, utilities, emergency)?

options.text: Single complex AI managing all sections.
Specialized AIs for traffic, utilities, and emergencies working together.
None, a manual system works best.
Any AI could manage a smart city just fine.

exercises.explain: Specialized AIs can address the unique needs of traffic, utilities, and emergencies more efficiently than one AI trying to do it all.
